─[ Bash set -e Return Guard ]─ ┐

name: bash-set-e-return-guard description: Avoid bare return in bash functions with set -e guard clauses user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Edit

Bash set -e Return Guard

When writing bash functions under set -e, guard clauses that use || return must specify an explicit exit code.

The Problem

A common pattern for early-exit guards:

set -euo pipefail

my_func() {
  [[ -d "$dir" ]] && find "$dir" -name '*.md' | grep -q . || return
  # ... rest of function
}

my_func  # Script dies here if guard triggers

When the condition fails, return (no argument) propagates the non-zero exit status from the failed test/command. Under set -e, this kills the script because the function call isn’t wrapped in if or ||.

The Fix

Always use return 0 for early-exit guards:

[[ -d "$dir" ]] && find "$dir" -name '*.md' | grep -q . || return 0

Snags

  • return with no argument returns the exit status of the last executed command, not 0
  • This only matters under set -e — without it, the non-zero return is silently ignored
  • The bug is invisible when the guard doesn’t trigger (happy path works fine)
  • Compound guards like [[ test ]] && cmd1 | cmd2 || return are especially tricky — if [[ test ]] fails, the && short-circuits with exit 1, and return forwards that 1
  • Functions called from if conditions or after || are immune to set -e, so the bug only appears in direct function calls
